description: Integration (DB): UI-origin ticket -> created + comment + closed; assert each outbound email_sending_logs row has a distinct rfc_message_id, all References share one root, the chain grows monotonically and is capped at root+20
Integration (DB): email-origin ticket -> anchor equals the inbound Message-ID; subsequent outbound emails carry In-Reply-To/References pointing at the inbound id
Integration/e2e (rig): customer replies to a NON-comment notification (e.g. status update) -> reply appends to the same ticket, no duplicate ticket created
Unit: References builder dedupes ids, preserves order, and caps to root + last 20
Unit: subject normalizer yields '[Ticket #N] <title>', is idempotent (no double prefix), and preserves 'Re:'
Unit: resolveTicketThreadAnchor returns inbound id for email-origin and a deterministic synthetic id for UI-origin, stable across repeated calls
Integration: SMTP (nodemailer) and Resend providers emit the exact Message-ID/In-Reply-To/References Alga set; recorded rfc_message_id equals the on-wire id
Integration (DB guard): when anchor/header resolution fails (e.g. missing email_metadata), the email still sends successfully without threading headers
Integration (DB): concurrent first-events on a brand-new ticket converge to a single anchor (no two distinct roots persisted)
